concerns.text: The plan cites 'VenusChenyy/RULER_50' (URL: .../131072.jsonl) as the primary source for 128k context tasks. However, the official RULER benchmark (HuggingFace: huggingface.co/datasets/ruler) is the canonical source for these tasks. User-uploaded mirrors (VenusChenyy) are not verified for integrity, versioning, or completeness in the spec's 'Verified Datasets' context. Relying on an unverified mirror for the primary evaluation suite violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and risks the 'Wrong Dataset' failure mode if the mirror diverges from the canonical RULER schema or contains corrupted data. The plan must cite the official RULER repository or a verified institutional mirror.

The plan assumes the 'MiniMax-M3' model in default (FP32/FP16) precision fits within 7GB RAM on a CPU-only runner for 128k context sequences. MiniMax-M3 is a large language model (likely 7B+ parameters). Loading a 7B model in FP16 requires ~14GB RAM just for weights, excluding the 128k context KV cache which can easily exceed 10GB. The plan's assumption that 'default precision is heavier but CPU-compatible' and fits in 7GB is factually incorrect for this model size and context length. This makes the proposed compute strategy infeasible as written; the plan must either specify a quantization method compatible with CPU (e.g., GGUF via llama.cpp) or reduce the model size/context length to match the 7GB constraint.
